content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


June 9, 1989


Leo F. Donahue
Attorney at Law
707 Commons Drive, Suite 201
Sacramento, CA 95825-6664

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-89-314

Dear Mr. Donahue:

You have requested advice on behalf of Walter J. Hernandez
concerning the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

QUESTION

Mr. Hernandez was employed by the State Fire Marshal's
office. His job title was "Chief, Hazardous Liquid Pipeline
Safety and Enforcement Division." Is Mr. Hernandez required to
file statements of economic interests pursuant to the State Fire
Marshal's conflict of interest code?

ANSWER

The position of "Chief, Hazardous Liquid Pipeline Safety and
Enforcement Division" is not designated in the State Fire
Marshal's conflict of interest code. Therefore, Mr. Hernandez is
not required to file statements of economic interests by virtue of
holding that position.

The State Fire Marshal's conflict of interest code was
adopted in 1977 and was filed with the Secretary of State on
November 3, 1977. There have been no amendments to the code since
it was initially adopted.


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Leo F. Donahue
Page Two


Enclosed for your information is a copy of Exhibit A
("Designated Positions") and Exhibit B ("Disclosure Categories")
from the State Fire Marshal's conflict of interest code.

If you have any questions, please call me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Jeanne Pritchard
Division Chief
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

Enclosure

cc: State Fire Marshal

Leo F. Donahue
Attorney at Law
707 Commons Drive, Suite 201
Sacramento, CA 95825-6664
Area Code 916
922-9242

FPPC

May 22, 1989
MAY 22 1989

RECEIVED

Colleen McGee
Political Reform Consultant
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: Walter J. Hernandez: Form 730, Statement of
Economic Interests

Dear Ms. McGee:

I represent Mr. Hernandez in an adverse personnel action taken by
his superiors at the State Fire Marshall's office. Mr. Hernandez
has been terminated because of alleged conflicts of interest
surrounding his Form 730.

Could you please tell me and supply the document, rule or
regulation that requires Mr. Hernandez, whose job title is Chief,
Hazardous Liquid Pipeline Safety and Enforcement Division, to
complete the Form 730 Statement of Economic Interest.

I understand that anyone who is required to fill out this form
must be registered with your office. If Mr. Hernandez is not,
and has not been, required to fill out this form, please so
indicate by yeas.

Obviously, it will be our position that, if Mr. Hernandez has not
been required to complete this form since 1984, then the
information gleaned from this form and used against Mr. Hernandez
should be suppressed with the effect that the conflict of
interest charges against him should be dropped.

If there are any procedures or remedies that I can pursue against
Mr. Hernandez' superiors through your office because of their
illegal requirement he complete the form, please so advise.

Thank you very much for your cooperation and consideration in
this matter.

Sincerely,

Leo F. Donahue
Attorney at Law

Secretary, State Board of Fire Services . . . . . . . . 1

Manager, Staff Services . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 2

-7-

EXHIBIT "B"

Disclosure Categories

An investment, interest in real property, or income is reportable if the business entity in which the investment is held, the interest in real property, or the income or source of income may foreseeably be affected materially by any decision made or participated in by the designated employee by virtue of the employee's position.

Designated employees in Group "1" must report:

A. Investments, interests in real property, income and all positions held as a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or other management position in any California business entity.

Designated employees in Group "2" must report:

A. Investments in any business entity regulated by, or subject to the jurisdiction of the Office of State Fire Marshal with a named vision or program.

B. Real property located in the jurisdiction of the Office of State Fire Marshal within the designated portion or program.

C. Use of any section as a Director, officer, partner, trustee, employee or any position management in any business entity regulated by, or subject to the jurisdiction of State Fire Marshal within the designated portion or program.

Designated employees in Group "3" must report:

A. Investments in any business entity which, within the last two years, has contracted, or in the other foreseeably may contract with the Office of State Fire Marshal or with the State of California to provide services, supplies, materials, machinery or equipment to the Office of State Fire Marshal.

B. Income from any source which, within the last two years, has contracted, or in the future foreseeably may contract with the Office of State Fire Marshal or with State of California to provide services, supplies, materials, machinery or equipment to the Office of State Fire Marshal.

-8-

OR FILING ADMINISTRATIVE REGULATIONS
WITH THE SECRETARY OF STATE
(Pursuant to Government Code Section 11353.)

C. His or her status as a director, officer,
partner, trustee, employee, or holder of a position of
management in any business entity which, within the last
two years, has contracted, or in the future foreseeably
may contract with the Office of State Fire Marshal or
with the State of California to provide services, supplies,
materials, machinery or equipment to the Office of State
Fire Marshal.

* * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * *

The State Fire Marshal hereby declares that
the foregoing regulations will not have a
fiscal impact upon local agencies and therefore
contain no reimburseable costs pursuant to
Section 2231 of the Revenue and Taxation Code.

-8-

FACE SHEET
FG. - FILING ADMINISTRATIVE REGULATIONS
WITH THE SECRETARY OF STATE

(Pursuant to Government Code Section 11320.1)

RECEIVED FOR FILING

OCT 9 1977

and correct copy of regulations adopted, or

MARCH FONG EU
SECRETARY OF STATE
OF CALIFORNIA

DO NOT WRITE IN THIS SPACE                DO NOT WRITE IN THIS SPACE

ORDER ADOPTING, AMENDING,
OR REPEALING REGULATIONS
OF THE
STATE FIRE MARSHAL

After proceedings had in accordance with the provisions of
the Administrative Procedure Act (Gov. Code, Title 2, Div. 3,
Part 1, Chapter 4.5) and pursuant to the authority vested by
Section 87311 of the Government Code, and to implement, interpret,
or make specific Sections 87300, et. seq., of Article 3, Chapter 7,
Title 9 of the Government Code, The State Fire Marshal hereby adopts
his regulations in Title 19, California Administrative Code, as
follows:

DO NOT WRITE IN THIS SPACE

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 24, 1989


Leo F. Donahue
Attorney at Law
707 Commons Drive, Suite 201
Sacramento, CA  95825-6664

Re: Letter No. 89-314

Dear Mr. Donahue:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on May 22, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division
